security_gap.gap_description: A flaw in the redemption logic could allow an attacker to redeem unowned $BGT or receive excess $BERA.

actor_risk.description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in Core L1 to illegitimately gain $BERA or affect $BGT supply.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting redemption logic can lead to theft of $BGT, unauthorized minting of $BERA, or breaking tokenomic invariants.

assumptions: The Core L1 smart contract responsible for $BGT redemption has a vulnerability (e.g., reentrancy, incorrect balance check, integer overflow/underflow in 1:1 conversion).
The attacker can craft a transaction to trigger this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorous security audits of Core L1 redemption logic and token contracts; adherence to secure token standards (e.g., ERC20 for $BERA, non-transferable aspects for $BGT); formal verification where possible.

description: An attacker exploits a flaw in the Core L1 redemption mechanism to redeem more $BGT than they own, or to receive more $BERA than the specified 1:1 ratio, thereby illegitimately acquiring assets or manipulating token supplies.
